message:
fix(runtime): normalize governor_interval=0 and expand env config coverage

RuntimeConfig::normalize() now clamps governor_interval=0 to 1, matching
the existing cancel_lane_max_streak normalization pattern.  A zero-tick
governor interval would cause the governor to fire on every scheduler
tick, defeating its purpose as a periodic rebalancing mechanism.

Updated the normalize tests to exercise this edge case: the zero-defaults
test now sets governor_interval=0 and verifies it becomes 1, while the
non-zero test uses governor_interval=7 and verifies it's preserved.

Environment config (env_config.rs):
- Added ASUPERSYNC_CANCEL_LANE_MAX_STREAK, ASUPERSYNC_ENABLE_GOVERNOR,
  and ASUPERSYNC_GOVERNOR_INTERVAL to the clean_env_locked() teardown
  list so they don't leak between tests.
- Added test: env_overrides_cancel_lane_max_streak
- Added test: env_overrides_governor_settings (enable + interval together)
- Added test: clean_env_locked_removes_governor_related_vars

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
